# signals

A learned classifier (trained on your runs, not a hand-written rule) that fires on matching runs; firings are events, grouped into clusters. Dashboard: Signals.

**When to use:** Listing or inspecting detectors, reading what a signal fired on, or registering and editing a signal record.

> **Footgun:** Binding a signal to a notification channel as a firing alert rule is dashboard-only; there is no `alert-rules` group.

## Commands

| Command | Invocation | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `create` | `bentolabs signals create --data '<json>'` | register a record (lands initializing); does NOT train a classifier |
| `delete` | `bentolabs signals delete <signal_id>` | soft delete: status to archived, stops firing, record kept |
| `get` | `bentolabs signals get <signal_id>` | one signal's full config + event_count |
| `list` | `bentolabs signals list [--limit --offset --source --status --group]` | all signals; filter by source, status, or group |
| `list-clusters` | `bentolabs signals list-clusters <signal_id>` | those firings grouped by similarity |
| `list-events` | `bentolabs signals list-events <signal_id> [--limit --cursor --start-time --end-time]` | the firings + what each matched on |
| `update` | `bentolabs signals update <signal_id> --data '<json>'` | edit name, prompt, or config |

Confirm exact arguments with `bentolabs signals <command> --help`.
